title: Taupo in July
meta_desc: Discover Taupo in July with kids: weather, events, and tips for a fun family holiday in the heart of New Zealand's North Island, with prices and age suitability.
intro: Taupo is a wonderful destination for a family holiday in July, with its mild winter weather and plenty of indoor and outdoor activities to enjoy. The town has a relaxed atmosphere, making it easy to unwind and have fun with the kids. With a budget of $200-350 per day for a family of four, you can experience the best of Taupo without breaking the bank.

sections.heading: Weather in July
sections.body:
July is winter in Taupo, but the weather is generally mild, with average highs of 12°C and lows of 2°C. It's a great time to enjoy the town's thermal pools and indoor activities. Be sure to pack warm clothing, including jackets and gloves, as it can get chilly in the mornings and evenings.

Rainfall is moderate in July, with an average of 12 rainy days throughout the month. However, this shouldn't deter you from visiting, as there are plenty of indoor activities to enjoy.

sections.heading: What's On in July
sections.body:
July is a great time to visit Taupo, with several events and festivals taking place throughout the month. The Taupo Winter Festival is a highlight, featuring live music, food stalls, and activities for all ages.

The Huka Falls are a must-visit, and in July, you can enjoy the scenic walkway and viewing platform without the summer crowds. The falls are free to visit and suitable for all ages.
list: Huka Falls viewing platform and walkway

sections.heading: Best Activities for Families
sections.body: For thrill-seekers, Skydive Taupo is an exhilarating experience, priced from $299 per person, suitable for teens and adults. The Huka Jet is a fun and action-packed activity, priced from $89 per adult and $59 per child.

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
July is a relatively quiet month in Taupo, with smaller crowds compared to the peak summer season. However, it's still a good idea to book your accommodations and popular activities ahead of time to avoid disappointment.

Drive times from nearby cities are: Auckland (2.5 hours), Rotorua (1 hour), and Wellington (4.5 hours). Be sure to check the road conditions before embarking on your journey.
list: Book accommodations and activities in advance

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
list: Pack warm clothing for the winter weather
Bring a stroller or carrier for younger children
Take advantage of the town's free activities, such as the Huka Falls viewing platform and Lake Taupo swimming
Consider purchasing a Taupo Fun Park pass for discounted entry to multiple attractions
Don't miss the free prawn feeding area at Huka Prawn Park
